zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Hill於1320年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根時，他的父親Sir William de Hill of Bridgnorth26歲，母親Lady Alice Baggard21歲。他於從1337年到1338年與Joan de Bassett在英國戴文艾可謝特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1368年在英國戴文艾可謝特享年48歲，埋葬在英國戴文艾可謝特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Hill于1320年出生在威尔士格拉莫爾根时，他的父亲Sir William de Hill of Bridgnorth26岁，母亲Lady Alice Baggard21岁。他于从1337年到1338年与Joan de Bassett在英国戴文艾可謝特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1368年在英国戴文艾可謝特享年48岁，埋葬在英国戴文艾可謝特。

nameMeanings.name: Hill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

variants.name: Hillock
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) small hill’, from Middle English hillok, a diminutive of hille (Old English hyll ‘hill’). See Hill .

variants.name: Hills
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hiller
meanings: German: metronymic from Hille, a pet form of the female personal name Hildegund (see Hilke ).
German: from a masculine personal beginning with the ancient Germanic element hild ‘strife, battle’.

variants.name: Hil
meanings: Dutch (Van den Hil): topographic name from hil, hul ‘hill’, particularly in the sense of a flight hill above sea level in the southwestern Netherlands.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Hill was born in 1320, in Glamorgan, Wales, his father, Sir William de Hill of Bridgnorth, was 26 and his mother, Lady Alice Baggard, was 21. He married Joan de Bassett from 1337 to 1338, in Exeter, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1368, in Exeter, Devon, England, at the age of 48, and was buried in Exeter, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Hill est né en 1320, à Glamorgan, Galles, son père, Sir William de Hill of Bridgnorth, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Lady Alice Baggard, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Joan de Bassett du 1337 au 1338, à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1368, à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterré à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Hill 1320 in Glamorgan, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William de Hill of Bridgnorth 26 und seine Mutter Lady Alice Baggard 21. Er hat von 1337 bis 1338 in Exeter, Devon, England Joan de Bassett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1368 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Exeter, Devon, England und wurde in Exeter, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Hill è nato il nel 1320 nel Glamorgan, Galles, il suo padre, Sir William de Hill of Bridgnorth, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Lady Alice Baggard, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Joan de Bassett da 1337 a 1338, nel Exeter, Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1368, nel Exeter, Devon, England, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolto a Exeter, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Hillが1320年にウェールズグラモーガンで生まれた時，父Sir William de Hill of Bridgnorthは26歳，母Lady Alice Baggardは21歳であった。から1337年まで1338年にイギリスデボンエクセターでJoan de Bassettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1368年にイギリスデボンエクセターで48歳で死去し，イギリスデボンエクセターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Hill(이)가 1320년 웨일스 글램르건에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William de Hill of Bridgnorth(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Alice Baggard(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1337년 부터 1338년 까지 영국 데폰 세터에서 Joan de Bassett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1368년 영국 데폰 세터에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 세터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Hill nasceu em 1320, em Glamorgan, Gales, seu pai, Sir William de Hill of Bridgnorth, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Lady Alice Baggard, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan de Bassett de 1337 a 1338, em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1368, em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra, com 48 anos, e foi sepultado em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Hill родился в 1320 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Sir William de Hill of Bridgnorth было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Alice Baggard, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Bassett с 1337 по 1338 в местности эксетир, девон, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1368 г. в местности эксетир, девон, Англия в возрасте 48 лет и был похоронен в местности эксетир, девон, Англия.

sm.from 1337 to 1338: mai le 1337 e o'o I le 1338

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Hill nació en 1320, en Glamorgan, Gales, su papá, Sir William de Hill of Bridgnorth, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Lady Alice Baggard, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Joan de Bassett desde 1337 hasta 1338, en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1368, en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultado en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra.
